_note: SYNTHETIC TEST DATA — generated by Claude standing in for a hiring manager, to test the Job Description agent's synthesis pipeline end-to-end. Not a real customer session.

openBrief.content: I want to hire the CIO for a pharmacy company.

answers.question: What is the title of the role?
answers.answer: Chief Information Officer (CIO)

answers.question: Where is this role located?
answers.answer: Paris, France. Hybrid — 3 days onsite. Must be France/EU work-eligible; no relocation support offered.

answers.question: IC or Manager? If Manager, how many direct reports and at what levels?
answers.answer: Manager. Leads an IT organization of ~45 people via 5 direct reports: Head of Infrastructure, Head of Applications, Head of Cybersecurity, Head of Data & Analytics, IT PMO Lead.

answers.question: Company details, mission, vision.
answers.answer: Mid-size European specialty pharmaceutical company (~2,500 employees), manufactures and distributes specialty generics across France, Germany, and Benelux. Mission: expanding access to affordable specialty medicine.

answers.question: Mission, values, culture.
answers.answer: Patient-first, scientific rigor, regulatory integrity, collaborative.

answers.question: Skills/culture taxonomy?
answers.answer: No formal framework yet — being built this year.

answers.question: New position or replacement?
answers.answer: Replacement.

answers.question: What worked well / didn't with the previous person?
answers.answer: Previous CIO (5 years) was strong on infrastructure stability but never modernized the data architecture. Board wants a digital-transformation-capable CIO now.

answers.question: How does this role contribute to strategic priorities?
answers.answer: Company is undergoing digital transformation to support EU regulatory reporting (EUDAMED, serialization) and data-driven R&D pipeline decisions.

answers.question: Examples of similar roles driving impact.
answers.answer: Not directly comparable, but the VP of Ops-led supply-chain transformation two years ago significantly improved visibility — the Board wants similar rigor applied to IT.

answers.question: Major goals/projects in next 6–12 months.
answers.answer: 1) Stand up a unified data platform for regulatory + commercial reporting. 2) Complete a cybersecurity maturity assessment and remediation roadmap. 3) Modernize the legacy ERP integration layer.

answers.question: Top 3–5 responsibilities.
answers.answer: Own IT/digital strategy & roadmap; lead cybersecurity & compliance (GxP, GDPR, EU MDR); manage IT budget (~€8M); oversee data & analytics function; represent IT to the Board/Exec Committee.

answers.question: Primary day-to-day activities and decisions.
answers.answer: Budget reviews, vendor management, cross-functional steering committees, incident escalations, architecture reviews.

answers.question: Processes/workflows/projects accountable for.
answers.answer: Change management, IT governance framework, disaster recovery/BCP, validated systems (GxP compliance).

answers.question: Authority/resources to execute.
answers.answer: Full P&L authority over the IT budget, hiring authority for the IT org, direct vendor contract approval up to €500K.

answers.question: Reports to? Dotted lines?
answers.answer: CEO, dotted line to the Board's Audit & Risk Committee.

answers.question: Manager's leadership style.
answers.answer: CEO is hands-off, outcome-focused, expects proactive escalation, low tolerance for surprises.

answers.question: Team size, structure, culture.
answers.answer: ~45 FTE across 5 functions. Currently siloed and somewhat risk-averse following a recent audit finding — needs a CIO who can rebuild trust and unify the org.

answers.question: Cross-department interaction; key stakeholders.
answers.answer: Works closely with Quality/Regulatory Affairs, Commercial, Supply Chain, and external auditors.

answers.question: Markets/segments/industries engaged.
answers.answer: France, Germany, Benelux — specialty generics, B2B (pharmacies, hospital groups), increasingly direct data-sharing with regulators.

answers.question: Regulatory/client/industry complexities.
answers.answer: GxP validated systems, GDPR, EU MDR/EUDAMED serialization requirements, health data handling — high regulatory bar.

answers.question: Expected seniority level.
answers.answer: Executive.

answers.question: Internal leveling framework placement.
answers.answer: Sits on the Executive Committee, same level as CFO/COO.

answers.question: Key skills/competencies/experience.
answers.answer: 10+ years IT leadership incl. 5+ in pharma/life sciences or another GxP-regulated industry; track record of digital transformation; strong cybersecurity/compliance background; experience managing multi-country regulatory reporting systems.

answers.question: Personal traits/mindset for success.
answers.answer: Comfortable operating in ambiguity, calm under audit pressure, able to rebuild a risk-averse team's confidence, high EQ working with the Board.

answers.question: Nice-to-have skills/experience.
answers.answer: Prior CIO/VP-IT title in generics or specialty pharma; experience with EUDAMED/serialization; fluent French.

answers.question: Examples of internal profiles who thrived in similar roles.
answers.answer: No direct internal comp, but the VP Ops who led the supply-chain transformation is viewed as the model of the right change-leadership profile.

answers.question: Career path this role could lead to.
answers.answer: Potential path to COO or continued Executive Committee track; could expand to group-level CIO if the company scales via M&A.

answers.question: Learning/development opportunities.
answers.answer: Executive coaching budget, Board exposure, conference/training budget for regulatory tech.

answers.question: Success at 12/24/36 months.
answers.answer: 12mo — unified data platform live for regulatory reporting; cybersecurity roadmap approved and underway. 24mo — full EUDAMED compliance achieved; IT team engagement scores measurably improved. 36mo — IT recognized as a strategic enabler of new market entry; no major audit findings for 2 consecutive cycles.

answers.question: How performance will be measured.
answers.answer: Quarterly Board IT report, audit finding trend, system uptime/incident SLAs, employee engagement survey for the IT org.

answers.question: Short-term wins expected (90 days / 6 months).
answers.answer: 90 days — complete an IT organizational assessment and stakeholder listening tour. 6 months — cybersecurity roadmap approved by the Board.

answers.question: Excellent vs. acceptable performance.
answers.answer: Acceptable = stabilizes IT and delivers projects on time. Excellent = proactively resets the Board's confidence in IT and turns it into a recognized strategic partner, not just a cost center.

companyInfo.benefits: Private health insurance, meal vouchers, hybrid work, annual bonus
companyInfo.deiPolicy: Formal gender-parity target on the Executive Committee; unconscious-bias training
companyInfo.applicationProcess: 3 stages: recruiter screen, panel interview with CEO + CFO, case study presentation to the Executive Committee

functionDescription: The IT function sits at Executive Committee level, ~45 FTE across Infrastructure, Applications, Cybersecurity, Data & Analytics, and PMO. Historically viewed as a cost center, now being repositioned as a strategic enabler of regulatory compliance and commercial growth.
